title: lichen_coap_request doubles timeout_ms without overflow check
description: Trigger: a caller supplies req->timeout_ms greater than UINT32_MAX / 2 to lichen_coap_request(). The function copies the user-provided timeout into uint32_t timeout_ms at lichen/subsys/lichen/coap/coap_client.c:371, then schedules the fallback timeout with K_MSEC(timeout_ms * 2) at lichen/subsys/lichen/coap/coap_client.c:376. The multiplication is performed in 32-bit unsigned arithmetic, so large values wrap to a much smaller delay. Impact: a request that was configured for a long timeout can time out almost immediately, invoking the callback with LICHEN_COAP_ERR_TIMEOUT while Zephyr's CoAP request may still be in flight. This is a correctness/resource-lifetime risk in the async request path. Fix: reject timeout_ms > UINT32_MAX / 2, clamp to a documented maximum, or compute in a wider type and range-check before K_MSEC().
close_reason: Completed: lichen_coap_request now rejects effective timeout_ms values above UINT32_MAX / 2 before scheduling the 2x fallback timeout, and the API comment documents the range.
